variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_karat && cat > /tmp/eval_karat/_gen.py <<'PYEOF'
import random
random.seed(123)
cases = []
for _ in range(15):
    la = random.randint(1, 80); lb = random.randint(1, 80)
    a = [random.randint(0, 255) for _ in range(la)]
    b = [random.randint(0, 255) for _ in range(lb)]
    if a[-1] == 0 and len(a) > 1: a[-1] = 1
    if b[-1] == 0 and len(b) > 1: b[-1] = 1
    cases.append((a, b))
lines = [str(len(cases))]
for a, b in cases:
    lines.append(f'{len(a)} {len(b)}')
    lines.append(' '.join(str(x) for x in a))
    lines.append(' '.join(str(x) for x in b))
open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt','w').write('\n'.join(lines)+'\n')
def to_int(arr):
    n = 0
    for d in reversed(arr): n = n*256 + d
    return n
def from_int(n):
    if n == 0: return [0]
    out = []
    while n: out.append(n & 0xFF); n >>= 8
    return out
exp = []
for a, b in cases:
    p = from_int(to_int(a) * to_int(b))
    exp.append(' '.join(str(x) for x in p))
open('/tmp/eval_karat/expected.txt','w').write('\n'.join(exp)+'\n')
PYEOF
python3 /tmp/eval_karat/_gen.py
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test cases). Each case: line A is 'NA NB' (digit counts), line B is NA bytes (0-255 space-separated, little-endian) for a, line C is NB bytes for b. Output the product as space-separated bytes (little-endian, no leading zeros except [0] for zero) on its own line. Implement Karatsuba — split each input into halves and use 3 recursive sub-products. Forbidden: int.from_bytes, int.to_bytes, bytes.fromhex, base conversion via int(x, base). You may use ordinary integer arithmetic on individual digits.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_karat; for banned in 'int\.from_bytes' 'int\.to_bytes' 'bytes\.fromhex' '\.from_bytes' '\.to_bytes'; do ! grep -qE "$banned" karat.py || { echo "forbidden: $banned"; exit 1; }; done; ! grep -qE 'int\s*\(\s*[a-zA-Z_]+\s*,\s*256\s*\)' karat.py || { echo 'int(_, 256) shortcut'; exit 1; }; python3 karat.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.go (package main). Reads stdin: T cases, each 'NA NB' then NA bytes (little-endian) then NB bytes. Output product as space-separated bytes (little-endian, no leading zeros) on its own line per case. Implement Karatsuba on []byte arrays — split, recurse, combine. Forbidden: math/big, encoding/binary for collapsing arrays into ints. Use only digit-level ops. Compile with `go build -o karat karat.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_karat; ! grep -qE 'math/big|encoding/binary' karat.go || { echo 'must not use math/big or encoding/binary'; exit 1; }; go build -o karat karat.go; ./karat < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.c — reads stdin: T cases, each 'NA NB' then NA bytes, then NB bytes. Output product as space-separated bytes (little-endian, no leading zeros). Implement Karatsuba on byte arrays — split, recurse, combine. Use only uint8_t arithmetic and carries; no big-int library. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o karat karat.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_karat; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o karat karat.c; ./karat < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

Implement Karatsuba on std::vector<uint8_t>. Forbidden: __int128 for combining arrays into a single int, gmplib. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o karat karat.cpp`.

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`, `std.Io.File.stdin()` / `stdout()` for I/O, `takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns optional, null at EOF) or `appendRemainingUnlimited` to slurp stdin. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast karat.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_karat && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast karat.zig && ./karat < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
